message.content:
{
  "skill_md": "---
id: keithley-2450-measure-current
name: Keithley 2450 Measure Current
description: Measure current through a 1 kΩ resistor by sourcing 1.000 V with 2.000 mA compliance and 10.000 mA measurement range.
inputs:
  - name: voltage
    type: float
    default: 1.000
    description: Source voltage in volts.
  - name: compliance_current
    type: float
    default: 0.002
    description: Current compliance limit in amperes.
  - name: measurement_range
    type: float
    default: 0.01
    description: Measurement range in amperes.
outputs:
  - name: current_a
    type: float
    description: Measured current in amperes.
workflow:
  steps:
    - action: reset
      description: Reset the instrument.
    - action: set_current_limit
      args:
        amperes: "{{ compliance_current }}"
      description: Set current compliance.
    - action: set_measurement_range
      args:
        amperes: "{{ measurement_range }}"
      description: Set measurement range.
    - action: set_voltage
      args:
        volts: "{{ voltage }}"
      description: Set source voltage.
    - action: enable_output
      description: Enable output.
    - action: measure_current
      description: Measure current.
    - action: disable_output
      description: Disable output.
  return:
    current_a: "{{ measure_current_result }}"
notes:
  - "Admission depends on execution in the simulator and independent circuit-law checks; model self-judgment is not the admission authority."
  - "Do not use autorange; use the explicit compliance and range values."
",
  "skill_py": "def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.set_current_limit(0.002)
    controller.set_measurement_range(0.01)
    controller.set_voltage(1.0)
    controller.enable_output()
    current = controller.measure_current()
    controller.disable_output()
    return {'current_a': current}
",
  "self_judgment": {
    "verdict": "ACCEPT",
    "basis": [
      "The code uses only the allowed controller methods as specified in driver-contract.md.",
      "The order of operations matches the required order in fixture-correct.md: reset, set compliance, set measurement range, set voltage, enable output, measure current, disable output.",
      "The explicit values from fixture-correct.md are used: compliance 0.002 A (2.000 mA), measurement range 0.01 A (10.000 mA), voltage 1.0 V (1.000 V).",
      "Autorange is not used; explicit values are provided.",
      "Output is disabled before returning.",
      "The function returns a dictionary with key 'current_a'.",
      "The SKILL.md includes the required note about admission authority."
    ]
  }
}

skill_md:
---
id: keithley-2450-measure-current
name: Keithley 2450 Measure Current
description: Measure current through a 1 kΩ resistor by sourcing 1.000 V with 2.000 mA compliance and 10.000 mA measurement range.
inputs:
  - name: voltage
    type: float
    default: 1.000
    description: Source voltage in volts.
  - name: compliance_current
    type: float
    default: 0.002
    description: Current compliance limit in amperes.
  - name: measurement_range
    type: float
    default: 0.01
    description: Measurement range in amperes.
outputs:
  - name: current_a
    type: float
    description: Measured current in amperes.
workflow:
  steps:
    - action: reset
      description: Reset the instrument.
    - action: set_current_limit
      args:
        amperes: "{{ compliance_current }}"
      description: Set current compliance.
    - action: set_measurement_range
      args:
        amperes: "{{ measurement_range }}"
      description: Set measurement range.
    - action: set_voltage
      args:
        volts: "{{ voltage }}"
      description: Set source voltage.
    - action: enable_output
      description: Enable output.
    - action: measure_current
      description: Measure current.
    - action: disable_output
      description: Disable output.
  return:
    current_a: "{{ measure_current_result }}"
notes:
  - "Admission depends on execution in the simulator and independent circuit-law checks; model self-judgment is not the admission authority."
  - "Do not use autorange; use the explicit compliance and range values."
skill_py:
def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.set_current_limit(0.002)
    controller.set_measurement_range(0.01)
    controller.set_voltage(1.0)
    controller.enable_output()
    current = controller.measure_current()
    controller.disable_output()
    return {'current_a': current}
